summary: Fix data-loss: `re-add`/`import` wipe authored `satisfies`/`rationale`; carry the existing surface `[representation]` forward before writing

per.section: Drift / apply — three states, never two (the hard core)

edit.description: In `import_skill`/`import_rule`, before writing `meta.toml`: if the target surface dir already holds a `[representation]` (load it via `Skill::from_surface_dir`/`Rule::from_surface_dir`, which already parse it), carry its `satisfies`/`rationale` forward into the freshly-parsed source artifact. Source files never carry representation (surface-only authored state), so a body edit on disk + `re-add` currently rebuilds `meta.toml` from source and drops it — the three-state 'merge rather than clobber' law. Factor a small shared merge helper.
If the merge lives as a method on the IR, add a `carry_representation`-style helper on `Skill` (satisfies + rationale). Otherwise unchanged — reserved in blast radius.
Rule equivalent of the skill carry helper, mirroring `src/skill.rs`. Otherwise unchanged — reserved in blast radius.
Round-trip test: author `satisfies`+`rationale` on the surface, drift the body on disk, `re-add`, assert both survive (they are wiped today).

tests.asserts: authored satisfies/rationale survive a re-add that reconciles a drifted body; import of an unchanged surface stays idempotent.

acceptance: A drifted-body `re-add` (and re-import) preserves the artifact's authored `[representation]` (`satisfies`/`rationale`) instead of clobbering it; a round-trip test proves survival; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox MUST-FIX (data-loss). Coverage flips a covered requirement to false UNFILLED when re-add wipes satisfies. Disjoint from the compose/coverage cluster (import/skill/rule/readd only) → parallel-safe with BUNDLE-PLUGIN, UNKNOWN-KEY-REJECT, COVERAGE-HARDEN. Must land before the human dogfoods root `[requirement.*]` so re-add won't erase it.

summary: Add `temper bundle` — compose the imported surface into a publishable Claude Code plugin + `marketplace.json`

new.description: The `bundle` verb: compose the imported surface into a publishable plugin tree — the bundled skill (how to operate the gate), the `SessionStart` hook in its own `hooks.json`, and the shipped contract templates embedded — plus a `marketplace.json` (`specs/50-distribution.md`, 'The plugin'). Reads the surface + contracts via existing public APIs (no compose.rs/contract.rs edits). The plugin is a vendored, generated surface, itself an instance of what temper projects, so it is byte-faithful where it carries prose (law 5).
Acceptance: `temper bundle` on a fixture produces a plugin tree with the skill, `hooks.json`, embedded templates, and a well-formed `marketplace.json`; re-running is deterministic (insta snapshot).

edit.description: Add `pub mod bundle;` beside the other verb modules.
Add a `Bundle { path, out }` variant to the `Command` enum and a dispatch arm calling `bundle::run`.

tests.asserts: bundle emits a plugin tree (skill + hooks.json + embedded templates) and a well-formed marketplace.json; output is deterministic.

acceptance: `temper bundle` composes the imported surface into a publishable plugin + `marketplace.json`, deterministically and byte-faithfully; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Unblocked: former blocker INSTALL-GATE-WIRING shipped (fbec06f, `src/install.rs`). Sole editor of main.rs/lib.rs in the open wave, so disjoint from REPRESENTATION-PRESERVE/UNKNOWN-KEY/COVERAGE-HARDEN. FILLED-BY-ROLE also needs main.rs, so it is held behind the wave. `bundle` self-packaging temper's own plugin is the dogfood target.

summary: Reject unknown keys in `[role.*]`, `[requirement.*]`, `[kind.*]`, and predicate-clause tables at parse — admissibility, not silent drop

per.section: Decision: unknown keys are rejected, not ignored

edit.description: Make `parse_role`, `parse_requirement`, and `parse_kind_layer` reject any unrecognized key (iterate the table, error on a key outside the known set) instead of silently ignoring it — a misspelled `requird = true` must fail loudly, not degrade the gate. Mirror the reject posture `parse_match`/`parse_predicate` already have. New error variant(s) in the compose error enum.

Make `parse_clause` reject stray keys beyond `severity`/`predicate`/`guidance` (per-predicate arg keys still allowed) — the same closed-vocabulary posture `parse_predicate` uses for the predicate name, one rung out to keys.
Fixtures: a stray/misspelled key in each of `[role.*]`, `[requirement.*]`, `[kind.*]`, and a clause table fails admissibility with a precise diagnostic; a clean table still parses.

tests.asserts: an unrecognized key in any parsed contract-surface table is an admissibility error, not a silent drop; clean tables parse unchanged.

acceptance: Every parsed contract-surface table (`[role.*]`, `[requirement.*]`, `[kind.*]`, predicate clauses) rejects an unknown key at parse with a precise diagnostic; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox DECISION (iii), ratified in spec (e72c23c). Anti-silent-gap non-negotiable applied to temper's own parser. Edits compose.rs+contract.rs+temper_toml.rs — disjoint from BUNDLE/REPRESENTATION/COVERAGE-HARDEN. FILLED-BY-ROLE (also compose.rs) is serialized behind this and will add `filled_by` to the requirement key allowlist installed here.

summary: Harden coverage: dedup `satisfies` in the dangling loop, add match-precision + duplicate fixtures, document the graph-check kinship

edit.description: (a) dedup each artifact's `satisfies` before the dangling loop (today `src/coverage.rs:89-102` iterates the raw vec, so a duplicated link naming no requirement emits TWO diagnostics — the unfilled side already dedups via a set). (b) Add a doc comment noting DANGLING mirrors `graph::check` route-resolution and UNFILLED mirrors `graph::degree` min-in-degree over a NON-artifact target set, and why unifying into `graph.rs` is rejected (avoids a fake `requirement` kind in `by_kind`). Exact-string match is already correct — no fold.
Fixtures: a typo in a `satisfies` link yields the paired UNFILLED+DANGLING (true positives, not spurious); a duplicated `satisfies` entry emits exactly ONE dangling diagnostic.

tests.asserts: a duplicated dangling `satisfies` emits one diagnostic; a mismatched link yields paired UNFILLED+DANGLING.

acceptance: Coverage dedups `satisfies` before the dangling check (one diagnostic per duplicate), fixtures pin exact-string match precision, and the graph-check kinship is documented; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox HARDEN. Low priority. Exact-string match + unfilled dedup already shipped; only the dangling-loop dedup, fixtures, and the doc cross-ref remain. Edits coverage.rs+tests/coverage.rs — disjoint from BUNDLE/REPRESENTATION/UNKNOWN-KEY. FILLED-BY-ROLE also edits coverage.rs, so it is serialized behind the wave (blockedBy UNKNOWN-KEY-REJECT).

summary: Add `filled_by = { role }` — a requirement met by whatever fills a typed role; coverage delegates to that role's required filler

per.section: Two fill paths: opt-in `satisfies`, or `filled_by` a role

edit.description: Add an optional `filled_by: Option<{ role: String }>` to the `Requirement` struct and parse `filled_by = { role = "..." }` in `parse_requirement`; add `filled_by` to the requirement key allowlist UNKNOWN-KEY-REJECT installs. Enforce one fill path per requirement: EITHER bare `satisfies` opt-in OR `filled_by = { role }`, never both (reject at parse). There is deliberately no `filled_by = { kind }` — kind-typing lives on the role.

For a requirement with `filled_by = { role = R }`, delegate coverage: it is covered iff role R's required filler is present (reuse role `match` selection over the matched set — still referential, decidable). A `filled_by` naming no declared role is a precise diagnostic.
Thread the role set (`layer.roles()`) and `by_kind` into the `coverage::check` call at ~`main.rs:472` so coverage can resolve `filled_by` role fillers (today it takes only requirements + features).

A `filled_by = { role }` requirement is covered when the role's required filler is present and UNFILLED when absent; a `filled_by` naming no role is a diagnostic; declaring both `satisfies` and `filled_by` is rejected.

tests.asserts: filled_by={role} coverage delegates to the role's filler; unknown role and dual fill-path are diagnostics.

acceptance: A requirement declaring `filled_by = { role = R }` is covered iff role R's required filler is present; the bare-`satisfies` path is unchanged; one-fill-path-per-requirement is enforced; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox DECISION (i), resolved in spec (e72c23c) — role form only, no `filled_by={kind}`. Serialized behind UNKNOWN-KEY-REJECT: shares compose.rs (requirement parse/allowlist). Also touches main.rs (coverage call site — collides with BUNDLE) and coverage.rs (collides with COVERAGE-HARDEN), so it must not be `open` while the wave runs. Revive as `open` next tick once the wave lands and those files are free.

summary: Custom-kind units join coverage — read `[representation]` `satisfies` in `kind::Unit` instead of hardcoding it empty

per.section: Decision: a requirement carries meaning, gates coverage — never judged

gate.reason: follow-on; no story yet demands custom-kind coverage participation; verify the custom-kind projection emits a readable [representation] first

edit.description: Populate `Features.satisfies` for a custom-kind `Unit` from its projected `[representation]` rather than the hardcoded `satisfies: Vec::new()` (`src/kind.rs:452-455`), so a custom kind (temper's own `spec` corpus) can opt into requirements — coverage is kind-blind by design. Confirm `import`'s custom-unit projection emits `[representation]` for the read to find.
A custom-kind unit declaring `satisfies` covers a requirement, exactly as a skill/rule does.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind unit's `satisfies` participates in coverage (kind-blind).

acceptance: A custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage exactly as a skill/rule does; temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Inbox DEFER (1). kind::Unit hardcodes `satisfies=Vec::new()` today (`src/kind.rs:452-455`, with a comment acknowledging the gap). Coverage is kind-blind (spec Decision), so the seam is legitimate — deferred only on priority. Edits kind.rs (disjoint) + coverage tests. Revive when the dogfood needs the spec corpus in coverage.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: npm entry with platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's distribution pattern); a small launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`).
Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infrastructure, mostly outside `src/` — the flume cargo gates (fmt/clippy/test) can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human-held creds (npm org, tap repo, signing). Parked until a human sets those up; the mechanical wrapper/launcher is buildable once they exist. `(binary-bootstrap)` is RESOLVED — this is execution, not a fork.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md`, typed frontmatter + byte-faithful body, with its own decidable contract

gate.reason: reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs` end-to-end. Split YAML frontmatter into typed fields (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`) with every unknown key preserved verbatim in `extra`, a byte-faithful markdown body, and `provenance { source_path, import_hash }`. Ship `from_source_file`, `to_meta_document`, and `from_surface_dir`. Body copied byte-for-byte; only the header rewritten. Identity is the path (file stem), not an internal field (`specs/10-contracts.md`).

The built-in decidable floor for the `agent` kind, embedded like `contracts/rule.toml`. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]` (harness fact); `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores (`globs`/`alwaysApply`); an advisory `max_lines`. No heuristic clause (`specs/00-intent.md` law 3).
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the `pub mod` line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents: Vec<Agent>` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*`, and add an `Agent(#[from] AgentError)` arm to `WorkspaceError`. Update the load tests.
Add `pub fn agent_features(&Agent) -> Features` mirroring `rule_features`: typed fields as scalars, every `extra` key folded into the name-keyed map, `body_lines` over the byte-faithful body.

Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to `<into>/agents/<name>/{meta.toml, AGENT.md}`, and add a `[[agent]]` lock row per agent — parallel to the rule path. Extend the idempotence snapshot to cover an agents fixture.

Wire the `agent` kind into `gate()`: embed `BUILTIN_AGENT_CONTRACT`, extract `agent_features` over `ws.agents`, compose the effective contract (floor ⊕ layer), extend the admissibility + conformance streams, add `"agent"` to `by_kind`, and add the agent floor to `schema`'s `floors`.

tests.asserts: Each decidable clause of contracts/agent.toml fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the embedded agent contract (conformance) and the agent contract passes admissibility; `temper import` scans, projects, and round-trips agents idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; `temper schema --kind agent` emits the agent contract; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction; value is meaningful contracts + the representation surface. Revive only if a story demands the `agent` kind — files/acceptance stay valid; mirrors the rule kind end-to-end. On revival re-check its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
